action.hypothesis: Crash recovery mode overrides the broader architecture search for this round, so the least-risk move is to stay on the strongest working family and make one minor parameter change that improves stability while preserving lower-K pressure. Running `lowrank_gated_residual` with the same stable `signum`/`LR=1.6e-3`/`AUXK_ALPHA=0` recipe at `EF=8` and increasing only `K` from the previously weak `40` to `64` should recover a clean proxy signal in the small-EF regime without reopening code or architecture risk.
action.summary: Use a stability-first proxy run on the current best working family by keeping `lowrank_gated_residual`, `EF=8`, `signum`, `LR=1.6e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0` fixed and changing only `K` to `64`. This stays within crash-recovery constraints, respects the small-EF guidance, and tests whether the allowed low-capacity regime can produce a valid, less brittle signal before any further architecture exploration.
action.expected_win: Recover a stable small-EF proxy measurement on a known working family and determine whether modestly relaxing sparsity from `K=40` to `K=64` meaningfully improves quality in the allowed `EF=8` regime.
action.self_review: Kept this round strictly param-only, used a known working architecture, avoided any code edits, and changed only `K` to satisfy crash-recovery and lower-K-first constraints as closely as possible while prioritizing stability.
notes_to_memory: Round 59 entered crash-recovery mode, so the round was constrained to `param_only` on a known working family rather than another architecture or code-path change.

Selected `lowrank_gated_residual` as the stability anchor and kept the stable shared recipe fixed while changing only `K` from `40` to `64` at `EF=8` to recover a valid small-EF signal under the current runtime constraints.

This round intentionally avoided `codebook_topk` follow-up tuning despite the repaired implementation because crash-recovery policy required a current best working configuration with minor parameter adjustment only.
next_hypotheses: If `lowrank_gated_residual` at `K=64`, `EF=8` is still clearly weak, treat the small-EF lowrank track as a stable negative result and pivot out of crash recovery only after the system shows another clean run.

If `lowrank_gated_residual` at `K=64`, `EF=8` is materially better than the prior `K=40`, `EF=8` result, continue the same one-variable K sweep within the allowed small-EF band before changing architecture again.

If this run crashes or shows runtime instability despite using a known working family, treat the issue as broader training-path instability rather than an architecture verdict and continue with stability-oriented param-only recovery.
